channel.title: 6 Minute Accessibility Digest
channel.itunes:author: 毒薔薇
channel.description: Daily conversations on digital accessibility and disability in art and design.

item.title: 2026-07-12 - Seattle disability arts fest, HHS exam-table mandate, overlay lawsuits
item.description: • Seattle's first disability arts festival and a new performance showcase put disabled creators center stage this week. • New HHS rules mandate accessible exam tables as overlay widgets keep drawing the lawsuits they promised to prevent. • Disability:IN's largest conference dedicates a full track to Human-Centered AI in workplace accessibility.

item.title: 2026-07-11 - DOE rule rollback, Oscar-honored captions, and MCA Chicago workshop
item.description: • Policy & Regulation: DOE moves to gut 50 years of building-access rules as China unveils a five-year accessibility plan. • Design & Technology: New Oscar-honored captions, EA's accessibility tags, and a push to build assisted-care spaces from the start. • Art & Culture: MCA Chicago's disability-led painting workshop reframes museum space through disability justice.

item.title: 2026-07-10 - DOJ deadline delays, disabled-led museum curation, and 2026 design agenda
item.description: • DOJ delayed government website accessibility deadlines even as ADA lawsuit filings keep hitting record highs. • Disabled artists lead curation at new Clark Art Institute and Toledo Museum exhibitions this month. • Architects and disability advocates set 2026's design agenda around universal access and "The World Works Better With Us."

item.title: 2026-07-09 - NFB's ADA lawsuit, V&A backlash, and new assistive AI tools
item.description: • NFB sues over the ADA Title II deadline delay as W3C's outcome-based WCAG 3.0 standard advances toward release. • V&A's disability design show draws mixed reviews as new gallery dis_place opens with disabled-led curation instead. • Google's Gemini Live Video and Apple's eye-controlled wheelchair steering expand real-time accessibility tools for users.
